description: Furniture editor enhancements — separate standing area for pet walk bounds, mouse-based drag/resize of collision and standing rectangles in preview, and parent furniture under existing Furnitures scene node

userStories.title: Add standing area properties to FurnitureData
userStories.description: As a furniture creator, I want standing_size_override and standing_offset properties on FurnitureData so walkable surfaces can be defined independently from interaction collision.
acceptanceCriteria: Add '@export var standing_size_override: Vector2 = Vector2.ZERO' to scripts/furniture_data.gd — when non-zero, defines the area where the pet can walk/stand
Add '@export var standing_offset: Vector2 = Vector2.ZERO' to scripts/furniture_data.gd — offset from sprite center for the standing area
Existing .tres files remain compatible — new properties default to Vector2.ZERO so current behavior is unchanged
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Use standing area for pet walk bounds at runtime
userStories.description: As a player, I want my pet to walk across the full defined standing area of furniture, not just the sprite-derived bounds.
acceptanceCriteria: In furniture.gd, get_surface_y() uses standing_size_override and standing_offset when standing_size_override != Vector2.ZERO — surface Y = global_position.y - standing_size_override.y / 2.0 + standing_offset.y + walk_surface_y_offset
In furniture.gd, get_left_x() uses standing area when defined: global_position.x - standing_size_override.x / 2.0 + standing_offset.x
In furniture.gd, get_right_x() uses standing area when defined: global_position.x + standing_size_override.x / 2.0 + standing_offset.x
When standing_size_override == Vector2.ZERO, all three methods behave identically to before (texture × display_scale)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add standing area controls to furniture editor form
userStories.description: As a furniture creator, I want form fields to configure the standing area in the editor, similar to the existing collision controls.
acceptanceCriteria: Add 'Custom Standing Area' checkbox in the Behavior section, visible only when Walkable is checked
When checked, show Standing Size W/H SpinBoxes (range 1–9999) and Standing Offset X/Y SpinBoxes (range -9999 to 9999)
When checkbox is first enabled, pre-fill with auto-calculated values (texture × display_scale)
Show auto-calculated label when unchecked (like collision auto label)
Standing area values are saved to .tres files via standing_size_override and standing_offset properties in _do_save()
Standing area values are loaded from .tres files in _load_furniture_data() — checkbox enabled when standing_size_override != Vector2.ZERO
_reset_form() clears standing area controls (unchecked, hidden, values zeroed)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Display standing area overlay in preview
userStories.description: As a furniture creator, I want to see the standing area visualized in the preview alongside the interaction collision area.
acceptanceCriteria: preview_display.gd gains standing_size and standing_offset properties
update_preview() accepts standing_size and standing_offset parameters
Standing area renders as a green semi-transparent rectangle (Color(0.2, 0.8, 0.2, 0.3)) with green outline
Interaction collision area continues to render in blue
Both rectangles visible simultaneously when both are defined
furniture_creator_panel.gd _update_preview() passes standing area values to the preview
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Mouse drag-to-move rectangles in preview
userStories.description: As a furniture creator, I want to click and drag collision or standing rectangles in the preview to reposition them.
acceptanceCriteria: preview_display.gd handles _gui_input for mouse events
Clicking inside a rectangle starts a drag operation that moves it
Dragging updates the rectangle position in real-time via _draw()
When drag ends, emit a signal with the new offset values (e.g., collision_offset_changed or standing_offset_changed)
furniture_creator_panel.gd connects to these signals and updates the corresponding offset spinboxes
If rectangles overlap, the smaller rectangle gets click priority
Cursor changes to CURSOR_MOVE when hovering over a draggable rectangle
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Mouse handle-to-resize rectangles in preview
userStories.description: As a furniture creator, I want to drag edge/corner handles on rectangles to resize them visually.
acceptanceCriteria: Each rectangle shows 8 small square handles (4 corners + 4 edge midpoints) when hovered or being edited
Handles are ~6px squares drawn on top of the rectangle outlines
Dragging a corner handle resizes from that corner (opposite corner stays fixed)
Dragging an edge handle resizes only in that axis
Emit signals with new size and offset values when resize ends
furniture_creator_panel.gd connects to signals and updates size + offset spinboxes
Minimum size enforced at 4×4 px to prevent zero-size rectangles
Cursor changes to appropriate resize cursor (CURSOR_HSIZE, CURSOR_VSIZE, CURSOR_FDIAGSIZE, CURSOR_BDIAGSIZE) when hovering handles
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Parent furniture under existing Furnitures scene node
userStories.description: As a developer, I want runtime furniture to be added under the Furnitures node already in main.tscn instead of a dynamically created FurnitureContainer.
acceptanceCriteria: In main.gd, replace dynamic FurnitureContainer creation with a reference to the existing Furnitures node in the scene tree (use $Furnitures or @onready)
Remove the _furniture_container = Node2D.new() / add_child / move_child code from _ready()
The Furnitures node in main.tscn may need to be changed from Node to Node2D if spatial parenting requires it
_spawn_furniture() and _spawn_furniture_at() add children to the Furnitures node (replace _furniture_container references)
Furniture renders behind the pet (Furnitures node is earlier in the scene tree than the pet)
All existing features work: edit mode highlighting, dragging, stacking, removal, passthrough rect calculation
Typecheck passes
